slide_1_Cover: KKokKKok: AI Eating Behavior Change Platform
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Sustainable Habit Formation : Breaks the binge-restrict relapse loop through sustainable behavioral change that persists after GLP-1 discontinuation — validated with 70+ participants at 80% completion
Economics : The AI + human coach hybrid handles 30+ clients per coach, suppressing labor costs non-linearly while delivering premium 1:1 relationship quality at 1:many pricing

Traction : Accumulated 10,000 active users and generated ~$27K+ in cumulative GMV purely through organic channels. Retention remains good, with a D30 of 12.3% doubling the health/fitness industry average
Meal Timer (satiety and emotion logging) encourages slower eating and visualizes mealtime patterns. This drives an average daily session time of 24 minutes and an industry-leading 12.3% D30 retention
Wearable eating speed detection uses smartwatch wrist sensors to analyze movement and deliver real-time slow-down nudges — passive intervention without opening the app
KKokKKok FIT 12-week coaching combines weekly 1:1 video sessions with daily AI feedback on meal journals to identify binge triggers and build mindful eating habits
10% of women globally have experienced an eating disorder — 79% of adults who restrict eating repeatedly fall into the restrict-binge-re-restrict loop
GLP-1 medications address appetite short-term but 68% discontinue within a year, regaining 2/3 of lost weight — a massive post-care gap no solution addresses
Diet and tracking apps see D30 retention of only 5-8% because they digitize calorie logging without addressing emotional and behavioral patterns behind disordered eating
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Korea represents the fastest GLP-1 adoption in Asia — Wegovy saw 400K prescriptions in 8 months; fewer direct post-care competitors than US or Europe enable faster beachhead
WHO positioned GLP-1 as part of chronic obesity management in 2025; 68% discontinue within a year creating structural demand for post-care behavioral support

The GLP-1 rebound is a physiological problem that crosses borders. Initial market tests prove global demand, achieving up to 20% CTR in the UK and 5-10% CTR in Japan, securing low top-of-funnel acquisition costs
Korea diet recovery market · yo-yo & binge eating
Global obesity drug and wellness market consensus: $95–150B+ by 2030 across Morgan Stanley, Grand View Research, and Goldman Sachs
GLP-1 adoption creates a downstream behavioral health market — patients who stop the drug need ongoing support the medication alone cannot provide
Structural Gap : 68% discontinuation rate (Prime Therapeutics) creates a structural post-care gap — millions of patients left without a maintenance solution
Relapse : Lancet STEP 1 data: discontinuers regain 2/3 of lost weight within 12 months — the behavioral gap is measurable and unaddressed
High diet awareness (86%) meets high yo-yo/binge prevalence (30%) in the world's most engagement-dense dieter segment
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: GLP-1 Companion launched with CBT coaching at scale; hundreds of clients per coach dilutes quality — no post-care focus
Integrated GLP-1 platform via Sequence acquisition; broad weight-loss positioning without focused eating behavior change
Virtual eating disorder treatment with 5-person care team; targets clinical ED patients, not GLP-1 rebound behavioral change
GLP-1 medication support app tracking injections and dosing with 3,470+ users; only assists during active drug use, no post-care
The Post Care Wedge : While Noom, WW, and Bbiyak focus on the active medication phase, Loop targets the critical gap between GLP-1 discontinuation and behavioral relapse.

Tracking "How" vs. "What": Competitors focus on quantitative calorie logging (what was eaten). Loop standardizes qualitative behavioral events—capturing the situation, emotion, and eating pattern (how and why it was eaten). This unique data feeds a proprietary AI learning loop, creating a defensive data moat over time
Launching in Korea provides a market with rapid GLP-1 adoption but significantly fewer entrenched behavioral competitors
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: App basic $7/mo; text coaching $39/mo; FIT 12-week intensive $157/mo ($471 total)
Freemium entry with organic YouTube/blog/open chat driving low-CAC acquisition
ACV ~$357/yr; high-engagement users reach $557-675/yr with coaching + maintenance
3-tier subscription staircase ($7 to $39 to $157/mo) with organic acquisition driving LTV/CAC improvement
Commerce : in-app store sells curated eating behavior support items (alternative tools, snack substitutes) expanding ARPU
Stage 1: Capture Korean female dieters aged 30-40 via 3-tier staircase — app ($7/mo) to text coaching ($39/mo) to KKokKKok FIT 12-week program ($157/mo)
Stage 2: Expand into Japan and UK where eating disorder awareness exists but behavioral care is lacking — ad response validated with 5-10% CTR (JP) and 20% CTR (UK)

Stage 3: B2B/B2B2C distribution — Partner with GLP-1 clinics to co-prescribe the platform as an essential post-care bundle. Concurrently, enter the corporate wellness sector (similar to Calm/Headspace models) to acquire bulk users and drastically reduce per-user CAC

AI-Driven Efficiency: CoachOS provides first-pass AI feedback on meal journals, reserving human intervention strictly for complex emotional cases. This hybrid structure enables a 30:1 client-to-coach ratio, suppressing labor costs non-linearly as the user base grows.

Proprietary Data Flywheel: Continuous coaching data feeds directly into the AI training loop. As the dataset expands, high-quality interventions become automated and reproducible, steadily decreasing human hours per user and structurally expanding gross margins.
B2B Volume Acquisition: Securing contracts with GLP-1 hospitals, pharmaceutical partners, and corporate wellness programs allows for the acquisition of hundreds of users per deal.
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Eunhyang is a 15-year eating disorder sufferer and 8-year OTT data engineer (1M+ MAU) who built KKokKKok from personal pain — designed the behavior event standardization and AI loop
COO Myeonghak trained 100+ clients as a certified PT (NASM-CPT/CES, NSCA) and runs YouTube 4K+, blog DAU 400+, binge recovery open chat — the organic engine that generated $25K+ revenue
CEO drives data-driven product decisions and AI pipeline; COO drives field operations, community, and coaching design — clear domain ownership with complementary skill sets
Married preformed couple who faced eating issues together — CEO insists on quantitative thresholds while COO catches qualitative field signals, creating productive complementary tension
OTT Data Engineer (8yr, 1M+ MAU): recommendation systems, 50GB to 20MB compression
15-year eating disorder sufferer; YouTube 'Anti-Diet Sosikchjwa' 350K+ views
Designs eating behavior event standardization, AI learning loop, and data pipeline
Certified PT (NASM-CPT/CES, NSCA): 100+ clients, 4-week and 3-month programs
POSCO Production Engineer (6yr): data-driven cost reduction of $714K+
Runs organic channels (YouTube 4K+, blog DAU 400+, open chat 1yr) for conversion
slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: A preformed married team joined Antler as 'kkoko FIT' with existing app (7,600 users) and coaching (70+ participants, $25K+ revenue) — CEO is 8yr data engineer; COO certified PT
Renamed to Loop during Reverse Thinking. Both share personal eating disorder experience — CEO as a 15-year sufferer, COO from training 100+ clients as PT
Married couple with complementary tension: CEO is data-driven (won't scale without proof), COO is field-driven (acts on qualitative signals) — productive disagreement
Bootcamp1 rejected with red signal — JaeHee feedback: trackout validation goals unclear. Problem definition not sharp enough for investment committee review
Bootcamp3-2 scored 4, green signal but rejected — customer understanding improving but not ready. Must prove experiential value translates to habit then payment
Bootcamp5 scored 5, approved for trackout April 8. JaeHee as IC sponsor. GLP-1 focus praised; hypothesis-experiment pairing still evolving
Group OH scored 7 post-trackout. Customer funnel built. Meal video analysis for 7 users secured. Pricing $39 (timer + video analysis + messaging)
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations
slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: Married couple, preformed as kkoko FIT. Shared eating disorder experience bonds them. Data vs field tension productive
7,600 users, 70+ coaching participants, $25K+ rev, D30 20.5%. Organic channels built over 9mo. Funnel post-trackout
Bootcamp1 red to BC3-2 (4, rejected) to Bootcamp5 (5, approved) to Group OH (7). Narrowed from broad diet to GLP-1 post-care
CEO 15yr sufferer + COO 100+ PT clients. GLP-1 post-care gap identified.
CEO 8yr data engineer (1M+ MAU OTT). CoachOS GPT-4.1 built. Core app outsourced initially; in-house transition early
New category: post-GLP-1 eating behavior change. How (emotion, pattern) vs What (calories). AI+coach hybrid 30:1 ratio
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: WHO, McKinsey, BCG, and JAMA all confirm that GLP-1 medications create massive downstream behavioral care demand. The 68% discontinuation and 2/3 weight regain rates are structural market failures, not temporary trends

The team combines lived experience with field expertise. The CEO is a 15-year eating disorder survivor who architected the behavioral tracking framework, while the COO is a certified PT with over 100 clients of field coaching experience

The company has accumulated 10,000 active users , ~150 cumulative paying customers , and ~$27K (₩37M) in cumulative GMV purely through organic channels, achieving a D30 retention rate (12.3%) that is double the industry average.
High-engagement coaching drives current revenue but is labor-heavy. If the human coach is the sole moat, this is a service business, not scalable SaaS
Claims regarding "binge reduction" and "eating disorder recovery" toe the line of medical Digital Therapeutics
Legacy players like Noom and WW are launching GLP-1 companions, while Equip targets clinical eating disorders
The current equity is heavily skewed at 90% (CEO) and 10% (COO)
● Current equity is 90% (Eunhyang) and 10% (Myeonghak), with plans to rebalance post-seed based on actual contributions and formalize governance (SHA, vesting, stock options)
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: 68% of users discontinue GLP-1s within a year, creating immediate demand for a $6.4B post-care behavioral market.
WHO officially positioning GLP-1 as part of a chronic management cycle that requires ongoing support
The broader weight-loss category is heavily crowded globally by well-capitalized incumbents like Noom and WeightWatchers
Loop has a differentiated wedge by targeting the specific post-medication discontinuation gap
Short-term defensibility is vulnerable because the high-engagement coaching that drives current revenue is labor-heavy, making it a service rather than highly scalable software
The long-term moat relies on collecting unique data (how users eat) to train the proprietary CoachOS AI
The team successfully executed a transition from one-off coaching to a scalable MRR structure in April, though absolute recurring revenue is still very early stage
The core problem (GLP-1 rebound) is a physiological, borderless issue rather than a localized cultural trend
Top-of-funnel global demand is highly validated (achieving up to 20% CTR in the UK), but down-funnel paid conversion and localization metrics remain unverified
Lack strategic sharpness. Early program feedback highlighted unclear validation goals and weak problem definition, requiring heavy external guidance to pivo

Strong founder-problem fit and a highly differentiated GLP-1 wedge make Loop an attractive early-stage bet. However, the core investment risk is scalability: the team must prove their transition from labor-heavy human coaching to a scalable AI + timer SaaS model to achieve venture returns
